title: Peppery Gooey Thai Skirt Steak Skillet
description: A juicy, pepperladen skirt steak seared in a creamy coconutsoy glaze, finished with a splash of lime and fresh herbs for a brunchready skillet dish.
instructions: Season the steak slices with salt and crushed peppercorns.
Heat a large skillet over medium‑high heat and add the vegetable oil.
Sear the steak for 2‑3 minutes per side until a dark crust forms; remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add garlic, ginger, and a pinch of chili flakes, sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in tamari, fish sauce, lime juice, and brown sugar; stir and let it simmer for 1 minute to deglaze the pan.
Add coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Whisk the cornstarch with water to create a slurry, then pour it into the skillet, stirring until the sauce thickens and becomes glossy (about 2 minutes).
Return the steak to the skillet, tossing to coat in the sauce; cook for an additional 2‑3 minutes until the steak reaches your desired doneness (recommended 140‑150°F for medium‑rare).
Stir in chopped basil and cilantro, removing from heat.
Serve hot, garnished with extra basil, on toasted bread or over steamed jasmine rice. Enjoy a peppery, gooey brunch dish!
